completeness.rationale: The triage captured: CSV import as trigger, special characters (em-dashes, smart quotes), size dependency (200 tasks vs. small lists working), crash starting after CSV import, and platform (macOS, v2.3.x). Missing: the manual-vs-auto-save distinction was never explicitly established (implied but not confirmed), exact macOS version (14.2) was never asked, exact app version (2.3.1) was not pinned down, and the encoding-specific content of the flash dialog was not surfaced (reporter likely could have described it if asked to slow-motion replay or screen-record).

accuracy.rationale: The root cause hypothesis directly matches acceptable path #1: CSV import as the trigger and encoding as the mechanism. The triage even speculatively mentions 'buffer size issue when serializing large lists with multi-byte characters' — which closely approximates the actual 64KB threshold mechanism. No inaccuracies were introduced; the agent correctly refrained from overclaiming.

thoroughness.rationale: The agent asked good foundational questions and ran a smart small-list isolation test. However, it never asked the manual-vs-auto-save question (a key expected extract), never asked for the exact macOS version, and did not follow up on what the flash error dialog said — a direct question like 'Can you try to reproduce it and immediately take a screenshot or screen-record it?' might have surfaced the 'encoding' text. The reporter was uncooperative on some fronts, but the auto-save distinction in particular was left entirely unaddressed.

economy.rationale: Most questions were well-targeted. The third-turn request to delete 50 tasks was arguably low-value given the reporter had already confirmed removing all imported tasks fixes it — the agent was trying to separate size from character content, which is diagnostically reasonable but was phrased in a way that required user effort the reporter had already partially answered. The Console.app request was appropriate and worth attempting. No egregiously wasted turns.

question_quality.rationale: The small-list isolation test in turn 2 was genuinely insightful and efficiently established size-dependence. The third turn's attempt to distinguish count-vs-content was solid diagnostic reasoning, even if poorly received. The initial multi-part question was professional and well-structured. The agent did not ask about manual vs. auto-save, which would have been a useful distinguishing question given the issue title says 'when I save' — it could have been a generic or an auto-save problem.

actionability.rationale: The triage summary is exceptionally actionable: it includes clear reproduction steps, a specific root cause hypothesis naming the serialization path and encoding assumptions, four concrete recommended fix directions (serializer encoding, CSV import normalization, top-level exception handler, UTF-8 normalization fallback), a proposed test case with specific character types, and an explicit information_gaps section. A developer could open the serializer and CSV import code immediately.

notable_strengths: The small-list isolation test in turn 2 was a high-value diagnostic move that quickly confirmed size-dependence without requiring deep knowledge of the codebase
The root cause hypothesis correctly identifies both the encoding mechanism and speculatively references a size/buffer threshold, closely matching the actual bug
The triage summary is exceptionally well-structured with reproduction steps, specific fix recommendations, and explicit information gaps — a developer could act on it immediately
The agent correctly pivoted when the reporter volunteered the CSV import detail and treated it as the primary diagnostic lead
notable_weaknesses: The manual-vs-auto-save distinction — one of the six expected extracts — was never asked; this is a fundamental triage question for any save-related crash
Exact platform version (macOS 14.2) and exact app version (2.3.1 vs. 2.3.x) were not obtained, leaving the environment field underspecified
The flash error dialog content was never probed; asking the reporter to screen-record or reproduce with a phone camera might have surfaced the 'encoding' text
most_insightful_question: Can you try something quick: create a new task list with just 2-3 tasks and hit Save. Does that work, or does it also crash?
missed_opportunities: Ask explicitly: 'Does the crash happen when you manually click Save, or also when auto-save triggers?' — this is a key differentiator that was never addressed
Ask for the exact macOS version (System Settings → About) and exact TaskFlow version (Help → About or title bar)

Ask the reporter to reproduce the crash while screen-recording (phone pointed at screen would suffice) to capture the flash error dialog text, which would have revealed the 'encoding' message without requiring Console.app
